title: Unexpected result when `RIGHT JOIN` with a subquery
description:
### What happens?

Consider the following test case. The query unexpectedly returns a row `2023-10-31 false`. If the expression `t1.c1 IS NULL` is evaluated as `false`, then the filter should be evaluated as `false` and the query should return an empty result.

MySQL or PostgreSQL can return the expected results.

### To Reproduce

```sql
CREATE TABLE t1 (c1 DATE);
INSERT INTO t1 (c1) VALUES ('2023-10-31');

SELECT t1.c1, (t1.c1 IS NULL)
FROM t1 RIGHT JOIN (SELECT NULL AS col0 FROM t1) AS sub0 ON true
WHERE (t1.c1 IS NULL);
-- Expected: empty
-- Actual: 2023-10-31 false

```

### OS:

Ubuntu 22.04

### DuckDB Version:

v1.3.0-dev1894 77849ba91c

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

SELECT t2.c1, (t2.c2 IS NULL) FROM t2 RIGHT JOIN (SELECT NULL AS col0) AS sub1 ON true WHERE (t2.c2 IS NULL);
CREATE TABLE t3 (c1 FLOAT, c2 BOOLEAN);
INSERT INTO t3 (c1, c2) VALUES (3.14, NULL);
key_bug_features: RIGHT JOIN with subquery returning unexpected non-empty result
